title: Yumurtlama Hesaplayıcısı — Doğurgan Günler ve Döngü
description: Adet döngünüze göre yumurtlama tarihinizi ve en doğurgan günlerinizi hesaplayın. Gebelik planlama takvimi.
ymyl_disclaimer: This calculator provides estimates based on a standard ovulation model. Individual cycles vary. This tool is not a substitute for medical advice or contraception. Consult a healthcare provider for fertility planning.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: How is the ovulation date calculated?
faq.a1: Ovulation is estimated to occur 14 days before the start of the next period. This timing is based on the luteal phase — the post-ovulation phase of the menstrual cycle — which is consistently approximately 14 days regardless of total cycle length. The formula is: Ovulation Date = Last Period Date + (Cycle Length − 14 days). For a 28-day cycle: 28 − 14 = 14 days after period start (day 14). For a 32-day cycle: 32 − 14 = 18 days after period start (day 18). For a 24-day cycle: 24 − 14 = 10 days after period start (day 10). The key insight is that cycle length variation comes almost entirely from the follicular phase (before ovulation), not the luteal phase.
faq.q2: What is the fertile window and why is it 6 days long?
faq.a2: The fertile window is the span of days during which unprotected intercourse can result in conception. It is 6 days long because: sperm can survive in the female reproductive tract for up to 5 days under favorable cervical mucus conditions, and the egg (oocyte) is viable for 12–24 hours after release from the follicle. The fertile window therefore spans from 5 days before ovulation through 1 day after. The highest probability of conception from a single act of intercourse occurs on the day of ovulation and the two days immediately preceding it — the 'peak fertile days.' This 3-day window accounts for the majority of natural conception.
faq.q3: How accurate is the calendar-based ovulation prediction?
faq.a3: The calendar method (used by this calculator) is most reliable for women with highly regular cycles. For women with cycles consistently between 26–32 days and less than 2-day variation cycle to cycle, the estimate is typically within 2–3 days of actual ovulation. For women with irregular cycles (common in PCOS, thyroid disorders, perimenopause, high stress, significant weight changes, or recent hormonal contraception discontinuation), the calendar method may be unreliable and actual ovulation can vary by 5–10+ days from the estimate. For higher accuracy, combine calendar prediction with LH surge testing (ovulation predictor kits) or basal body temperature tracking.
faq.q4: What is an LH surge and how do ovulation predictor kits work?
faq.a4: Luteinizing hormone (LH) is produced by the pituitary gland and triggers ovulation approximately 24–36 hours after a sharp rise in blood and urine levels — the 'LH surge.' Ovulation predictor kits (OPKs) detect this surge in urine. When a test line is as dark or darker than the control line, the LH surge is occurring and ovulation is expected within 12–36 hours — the optimal window to time intercourse for conception. Digital OPKs (like Clearblue) display 'peak' or 'high' readings to simplify interpretation. LH testing is significantly more accurate than the calendar method alone, particularly for women with irregular cycles.
faq.q5: Can I use this calculator to avoid pregnancy?
faq.a5: This calculator is not designed or reliable for contraception. The calendar method (fertility awareness method) has a typical-use failure rate of approximately 24% per year — meaning nearly 1 in 4 women using it for contraception will become pregnant within a year. Cycle variability, missed periods, and stress-induced ovulation shifts make calendar-only contraception highly unreliable. The only reliable forms of contraception are barrier methods (condoms 85–98% effective), hormonal contraception (pill, patch, ring, shot, implant: 91–99% effective), or IUDs (>99%). Always consult a healthcare provider for contraception guidance.
faq.q6: What is basal body temperature (BBT) and how does it confirm ovulation?
faq.a6: Basal body temperature (BBT) is your body temperature measured at rest, immediately upon waking before any activity. After ovulation, progesterone production causes a slight rise in BBT of 0.2–0.5°C (0.4–0.9°F) that persists until the next period. By tracking BBT daily with a specialized digital thermometer, a pattern emerges that confirms ovulation has occurred — the 'thermal shift.' BBT tracking confirms ovulation retrospectively (you can see it happened) but does not predict it in advance. Used together with calendar estimation and LH testing, BBT tracking creates the most complete picture of your cycle for fertility planning.
faq.q7: How does polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) affect ovulation prediction?
faq.a7: PCOS is the most common cause of anovulation (irregular or absent ovulation) and affects approximately 5–10% of women of reproductive age. PCOS cycles are often irregular, ranging from fewer than 21 days to more than 35 days or being completely absent. Ovulation may occur at unpredictable times or not at all in a given cycle. The calendar method is not reliable for women with PCOS — actual ovulation may occur days or weeks later or earlier than predicted. Women with PCOS who are trying to conceive should work with a reproductive endocrinologist and may benefit from cycle-inducing medications (letrozole, clomiphene), timed ovarian monitoring via ultrasound, or IVF.
faq.q8: What symptoms suggest I may be ovulating?
faq.a8: Several physical signs accompany ovulation, though their presence and intensity vary by individual: Cervical mucus changes — around ovulation, discharge becomes clear, stretchy, and slippery ('egg white consistency'), resembling raw egg white. This fertile-quality cervical mucus is essential for sperm survival and transport. Mid-cycle spotting — light pink or brown spotting around ovulation occurs in approximately 5% of cycles. Mild pelvic pain — Mittelschmerz ('middle pain' in German) is a one-sided cramping or aching sensation some women feel at ovulation, caused by follicle rupture. Breast tenderness — hormonal changes around ovulation can cause mild breast sensitivity. Increased libido — biological drive for intercourse increases around the fertile window in many women. These signs, combined with calendar and LH testing, form a comprehensive fertility awareness picture.

content.whatIs:
Yumurtlama hesaplayıcı, son adet tarihinizin ilk günü ve ortalama döngü uzunluğunuza dayalı olarak yumurtlama tarihinizi, doğurganlık pencerenizi ve bir sonraki adet tarihinizi tahmin eder. Doğal gebelik veya aile planlaması farkındalığı için en olası pencereyi vermek amacıyla standart üreme endokrinolojisi modelini uygular — yumurtlama, bir sonraki adetten 14 gün önce gerçekleşir ve doğurganlık penceresi 5 gün öncesinden 1 gün sonrasına uzanır.

Döngünüzü anlamak, ister gebe kalmaya çalışıyor, ister gebelikten kaçınıyor, ister yalnızca kendi biyolojiniz hakkında farkındalık kazanıyor olun, üreme sağlığının temelidir. Adet döngüsü iki aşamaya bölünür: foliküler faz (adet başlangıcından yumurtlamaya kadar) ve luteal faz (yumurtlamadan bir sonraki adete kadar). Luteal faz, döngü uzunluğundan bağımsız olarak çoğu kadında yaklaşık 14 günde dikkate değer biçimde tutarlıdır. Bu, döngü uzunluğu varyasyonunun — 24 günlük veya 35 günlük döngüye sahip olmanız fark etmez — neredeyse tamamen foliküler fazdaki farklılıklardan kaynaklandığı anlamına gelir; luteal fazdan değil. Bu, hesaplayıcının formülünün biyolojik temelidir.

Bu hesaplayıcı, düzenli döngülere sahip kadınlar için en doğru olan takvim tabanlı tahminler sağlar. Gerçek doğurganlık farkındalığı, maksimum doğruluk için takvim tahminini yumurtlamanın fiziksel belirtileriyle (serviks mukusu değişiklikleri, bazal vücut sıcaklığı) ve LH dalgası testleriyle birleştirmekten yararlanır. Burada verilen tarihleri başlangıç çerçevesi olarak düşünün — gerçek doğurganlık penceresi, birden fazla gözlemi birleştirerek doğrulanır.
content.formula:
Döngüdeki Yumurtlama Günü = Döngü Uzunluğu − 14
Yumurtlama Tarihi = Son Adet Tarihi + Yumurtlama Günü

Doğurganlık Penceresi:
  Başlangıç = Yumurtlama Tarihi − 5 gün  (sperm yaşam süresi)
  Bitiş     = Yumurtlama Tarihi + 1 gün   (yumurta canlılığı)
  Pik doğurganlık günleri = Yumurtlama Tarihi − 2'den Yumurtlama Tarihine

Bir Sonraki Adet Tarihi = Son Adet Tarihi + Döngü Uzunluğu

Yumurtlamaya Kadar Gün Sayısı = Yumurtlama Tarihi − Bugün
  (negatif = bu döngüde yumurtlama zaten geçti)

Örnek döngü uzunlukları ve yumurtlama günleri:
  24 günlük döngü → 10. günde yumurtlama
  26 günlük döngü → 12. günde yumurtlama
  28 günlük döngü → 14. günde yumurtlama (standart)
  30 günlük döngü → 16. günde yumurtlama
  32 günlük döngü → 18. günde yumurtlama
  35 günlük döngü → 21. günde yumurtlama
content.example:
Örnek 1 — Standart 28 günlük döngü:
Son adet: 1 Ocak 2025
Yumurtlama günü: 28 − 14 = 14. gün → 15 Ocak 2025
Doğurganlık penceresi: 10–16 Ocak 2025
Bir sonraki adet: 29 Ocak 2025
Yumurtlamaya kadar gün (10 Oca'dan itibaren): 5 gün
Pik doğurganlık (en yüksek gebelik şansı): 13–15 Ocak

Örnek 2 — Daha uzun 32 günlük döngü:
Son adet: 1 Ocak 2025
Yumurtlama günü: 32 − 14 = 18. gün → 19 Ocak 2025
Doğurganlık penceresi: 14–20 Ocak 2025
Bir sonraki adet: 2 Şubat 2025

Örnek 3 — Kısa 24 günlük döngü:
Son adet: 1 Ocak 2025
Yumurtlama günü: 24 − 14 = 10. gün → 11 Ocak 2025
Doğurganlık penceresi: 6–12 Ocak 2025
Not: doğurganlık penceresinin başında adet kanaması hâlâ devam ediyor olabilir.
content.howItWorks_1: Son adet tarihinizin ilk gününü girin — spotting değil, gerçek akışın başladığı ilk gün. Bu, tüm hesaplamalar için çapa tarihidir. Tam tarihi bilmiyorsanız, adet takip uygulamanızdan veya takvimden mevcut en iyi tarihi kullanın.
content.howItWorks_2: Ortalama döngü uzunluğunuzu gün cinsinden girin. Bir adet döngüsü, bir adetten bir sonrakinin başlangıcından bir önceki güne kadar sayılır. Tipik döngü uzunlukları 21 ile 35 gün arasında değişmekte olup tıbbi literatürde ortalama 28 gündür. Döngüleriniz önemli ölçüde değişiyorsa, son 3–6 döngünüzün ortalamasını kullanın. Referans olarak tek bir olağandışı kısa veya uzun döngü kullanmaktan kaçının.
content.howItWorks_3: Tahmini yumurtlama tarihinizi, tam doğurganlık penceresini (başlangıç ve bitiş tarihleri), bir sonraki beklenen adet tarihini ve bugünden itibaren yumurtlamaya kaç gün kaldığını görmek için Hesapla'ya tıklayın. Yumurtlamaya kadar gün sayısı negatifse, yumurtlamanın bu döngüde zaten geçtiği tahmin edilmektedir — sonuçlar yine de bu döngünün tarihlerini gösterir ve bir sonraki adet tarihi yeni döngünüzün ne zaman başlayacağını söyler.
content.whyItMatters:
Fertility awareness — understanding when ovulation occurs — is one of the most practically useful pieces of reproductive health knowledge available to people with cycles. For those trying to conceive, accurately identifying the fertile window significantly reduces the time to conception. Research shows that couples who time intercourse to the fertile window (particularly the 2–3 days before and including ovulation) have conception rates 2–3× higher per cycle compared to random timing. Given that the average fertile window is only 6 days per cycle, and most of a cycle is infertile, random timing has limited efficiency.

The psychological benefits of fertility awareness are also significant. For the approximately 1 in 8 couples who experience difficulty conceiving, having a clear understanding of the cycle can reduce anxiety, prevent misattribution of infertility (e.g., blaming physiology when the issue was timing), and guide more productive conversations with reproductive health providers. Many couples seeking infertility consultations are not aware that human conception is only possible on 6 days per cycle, and some have been having intercourse well outside their fertile window entirely.

Beyond conception, cycle awareness has broader health applications. Tracking cycle regularity allows early detection of cycle changes that may indicate hormonal imbalances, thyroid disorders, PCOS, or perimenopause. The menstrual cycle is increasingly recognized by endocrinologists as a 'fifth vital sign' — a regular, predictable biological process whose disruption signals potential systemic health issues. A woman who knows her normal cycle length of 28 days can quickly identify when a 35-day or 42-day cycle is abnormal and merits investigation.
content.limitations:
The calendar method used by this calculator assumes a consistent luteal phase of exactly 14 days. In reality, the luteal phase ranges from 10 to 16 days in healthy women, with an average of 14 days. A shorter-than-typical luteal phase (luteal phase defect, fewer than 10 days) can affect fertility and is not detectable by this calculator. Additionally, the follicular phase varies not just between women but in the same woman cycle to cycle — stress, illness, travel, weight changes, and significant exercise can all shift ovulation timing by several days.

This calculator does not adjust for: irregular cycles, absent ovulation (anovulation), recent discontinuation of hormonal contraception (which can take 2–3 months for cycles to normalize), breastfeeding (which often suppresses ovulation), thyroid disorders, or PCOS. For all of these conditions, calendar-based prediction is substantially less reliable than for women with regular cycles, and clinical fertility monitoring is recommended.

This tool is not a medical device and should not replace clinical evaluation for anyone experiencing infertility. If you have been trying to conceive for 12 months without success (6 months if over age 35), referral to a reproductive endocrinologist is the standard recommendation. Testing for both partners (semen analysis for male, hormonal assessment and cycle monitoring for female) provides the most complete picture and allows targeted intervention.
content.tips_1: For the highest conception probability, plan intercourse on the 2–3 days leading up to and including the estimated ovulation date — not after. Sperm survive up to 5 days in fertile cervical mucus, so early-in-the-window timing is highly effective. The egg is viable for only 12–24 hours after ovulation — waiting until after ovulation to time intercourse significantly reduces the probability per cycle.
content.tips_2: Combine this calendar estimate with ovulation predictor kit (OPK) testing for significantly higher accuracy. Begin testing urine 2 days before the estimated ovulation date (in the early afternoon, not morning — LH peaks later in the day). A positive OPK (test line as dark or darker than control) indicates the LH surge and predicts ovulation within 12–36 hours. This real-time biological confirmation eliminates calendar estimation error entirely.
content.tips_3: Track cervical mucus changes as an additional free, natural fertility sign. In the days before ovulation, discharge changes from dry or sticky (infertile) to creamy, then to clear, stretchy, and slippery (fertile). This 'egg white cervical mucus' (EWCM) is the most visible sign that the fertile window is open and the body is approaching ovulation. Observing EWCM combined with this calculator's prediction and OPK testing creates a three-layer confirmation of peak fertility.
content.tips_4: If you have irregular cycles or have been trying to conceive for 3+ months without success, see a gynecologist or reproductive endocrinologist before further calendar-based attempts. Blood tests on day 3 (FSH, LH, estradiol, AMH) assess ovarian reserve. A day-21 progesterone test confirms whether ovulation occurred. Cycle monitoring via transvaginal ultrasound can directly visualize follicle development and confirm ovulation with certainty — information no calculator can provide.
